name: Malaria
description: A parasitic disease caused by Plasmodium parasites, spread through bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. One of the leading causes of illness and death in Uganda.
spreads: Through bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. Not spread person to person.
prevention: Sleep under an insecticide-treated mosquito net every night
Use mosquito repellent on exposed skin especially at dusk and dawn
Eliminate standing water around your home where mosquitoes breed
urgency_reason: Malaria can become fatal within 24 hours if untreated. Go to a health facility immediately.

name: Typhoid Fever
description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water. Very common in areas with poor sanitation.
spreads: Through eating or drinking food and water contaminated with the faeces of an infected person.
prevention: Drink only boiled or treated water
Wash hands thoroughly with soap before eating and after using the toilet
Avoid eating street food that may be contaminated
urgency_reason: See a clinic within 24 hours. Typhoid requires antibiotic treatment and worsens without it.

name: Cholera
description: A severe bacterial infection of the intestine caused by Vibrio cholerae. Causes extreme watery diarrhoea that leads to dangerous dehydration very quickly.
spreads: Through contaminated water and food. Spreads rapidly in areas without clean water and proper sanitation.
prevention: Only drink water that has been boiled or treated with chlorine
Wash hands with soap and clean water before handling food
Cook food thoroughly and eat it while still hot
urgency_reason: Cholera can kill within hours through dehydration. Go to hospital immediately.

name: Tuberculosis
description: A bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis that mainly affects the lungs. Uganda has one of the highest TB burdens in Africa.
spreads: Through the air when an infected person coughs, sneezes or speaks. Prolonged close contact is usually needed.
prevention: Ensure good ventilation in your home and workplace
Cover your mouth and nose when coughing or sneezing
Get the BCG vaccine which protects children against severe TB
urgency_reason: TB is curable but requires a full 6 month course of antibiotics. See a clinic for a TB test.

name: HIV/AIDS
description: A viral infection caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus that attacks the immune system. Uganda has made major progress in treatment but new infections still occur.
spreads: Through unprotected sexual contact, sharing needles, blood transfusion, or from mother to child during birth or breastfeeding.
prevention: Use condoms consistently during sexual intercourse
Get tested regularly especially if sexually active
Never share needles or sharp instruments
urgency_reason: HIV is manageable with antiretroviral therapy. Visit a health centre for a confidential test.

name: Bilharzia (Schistosomiasis)
description: A disease caused by parasitic worms that live in fresh water. It is common in fishing communities and areas near lakes or rivers.
spreads: Through skin contact with contaminated fresh water where certain types of snails live.
prevention: Avoid swimming or wading in fresh water known to be contaminated
Boil water for bathing or let it stand for 2 days before use
Use improved latrines to prevent water contamination
urgency_reason: Chronic infection can lead to liver damage, kidney failure, or infertility if left untreated over time.

name: Sleeping Sickness (Human African Trypanosomiasis)
description: A parasitic disease transmitted by the tsetse fly. If untreated, it eventually invades the central nervous system.
spreads: Through the bite of an infected tsetse fly.
prevention: Wear long-sleeved clothing of medium-weight material in neutral colors
Use insect repellent
Avoid bushes or areas where tsetse flies are known to breed
urgency_reason: The disease is almost always fatal without treatment as it eventually attacks the brain.

name: Measles
symptoms: tiny white spots inside the mouth
description: A highly contagious viral disease that remains a significant cause of death among young children.
spreads: Airborne droplets through coughing, sneezing, or close personal contact.
prevention: Ensure children receive two doses of the measles vaccine
Isolate infected individuals to prevent community spread
Practice good respiratory hygiene
urgency_reason: Measles can lead to severe complications like pneumonia, brain swelling (encephalitis), and blindness.

name: Meningitis
description: An inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord, often caused by bacterial or viral infection.
spreads: Direct contact, including sneezing, coughing, or sharing utensils with an infected person.
prevention: Get vaccinated against meningococcal and pneumococcal bacteria
Wash hands frequently
Avoid sharing drinks or food with sick individuals
urgency_reason: Bacterial meningitis can be fatal within hours. Immediate medical attention is required.

name: Rabies
description: A viral disease of the central nervous system usually transmitted through the bite of an infected animal.
spreads: Through the saliva of infected animals, usually via bites or scratches (most commonly dogs).
prevention: Vaccinate pets (dogs and cats) annually
Avoid contact with wild or stray animals
Wash any bite wound immediately with soap and water for 15 minutes
urgency_reason: Once clinical symptoms appear, rabies is 100% fatal. Post-exposure vaccination must start immediately after a bite.

name: Hepatitis B
description: A viral infection that attacks the liver and can cause both acute and chronic disease.
spreads: Contact with blood or other body fluids of an infected person; mother to child at birth.
prevention: Receive the Hepatitis B vaccine series
Avoid sharing needles, razors, or toothbrushes
Practice safe sex and ensure sterile medical/tattoo equipment
urgency_reason: While often asymptomatic early on, it can lead to cirrhosis and liver cancer if unmonitored.

name: Brucellosis
description: A bacterial disease that spreads from animals to humans, often known as 'Malta fever'.
spreads: Eating contaminated food (unpasteurized milk/cheese) or direct contact with infected animal fluids.
prevention: Pasteurize all dairy products before consumption
Cook meat thoroughly
Wear protective gear when handling livestock or animal carcasses
urgency_reason: Can cause long-term complications like arthritis or swelling of the heart and liver if not treated with antibiotics.

name: Pneumonia
description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid.
spreads: Inhaling respiratory droplets from coughs or sneezes of an infected person.
prevention: Vaccination (PCV and Flu vaccines)
Frequent handwashing
Reducing indoor air pollution (like wood smoke)
urgency_reason: Can lead to respiratory failure and is a leading cause of death in children under 5.

name: Dengue Fever
description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing a severe flu-like illness.
spreads: Bites of infected Aedes aegypti mosquitoes.
prevention: Use mosquito repellent
Wear long-sleeved clothing
Empty containers of standing water to prevent mosquito breeding
urgency_reason: Can progress to Severe Dengue (hemorrhagic fever), which causes internal bleeding and shock.

name: Dysentery
description: An intestinal inflammation, primarily of the colon, that can be caused by bacteria (Shigella) or amoebas.
spreads: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food, water, or dirty hands.
prevention: Wash hands with soap after using the toilet and before eating
Drink safe, treated, or boiled water
Ensure food is cooked and handled hygienically
urgency_reason: Rapid dehydration and bloodstream infections can be life-threatening, especially for children.

name: Rift Valley Fever (RVF)
description: A viral zoonosis that primarily affects animals but can also infect humans.
spreads: Contact with blood or organs of infected animals; occasionally through mosquito bites.
prevention: Wear gloves and protective gear when handling sick animals
Avoid consuming raw milk or undercooked meat from livestock
Use mosquito nets and repellent
urgency_reason: A small percentage of cases develop into severe disease involving eye lesions, encephalitis, or hemorrhagic fever.

name: Sickle Cell Crisis
description: A painful episode that occurs when sickle-shaped red blood cells block blood flow to organs.
spreads: Genetic; inherited from parents. Not infectious.
prevention: Stay hydrated and avoid extreme temperatures
Attend regular medical checkups and take prescribed folic acid
Prevent infections through vaccination and hygiene
urgency_reason: Can lead to organ damage, stroke, or severe anemia requiring urgent blood transfusion.

name: Severe Malnutrition
description: A condition where the body does not get enough nutrients to function, often seen as Marasmus or Kwashiorkor.
spreads: Not infectious; caused by inadequate diet or repeated infections.
prevention: Exclusive breastfeeding for the first 6 months
Diverse diet including proteins, vitamins, and minerals
Regular growth monitoring and deworming for children
urgency_reason: Weakens the immune system making common infections fatal; can cause permanent physical and mental stunting.

name: Hypertension Crisis
description: A severe increase in blood pressure that can lead to a stroke or heart attack.
spreads: Non-communicable; related to genetics and lifestyle.
prevention: Reduce salt intake
Regular exercise and maintaining a healthy weight
Take prescribed blood pressure medication consistently
urgency_reason: Can cause immediate organ failure, stroke, or heart attack. Requires emergency medical care.

name: Snake Bite
description: An injury caused by a bite from a snake, which may or may not involve venom (envenomation).
spreads: Direct contact with a snake.
prevention: Wear boots and long trousers when walking in tall grass
Use a torch at night
Keep homes and compounds clear of debris and rodents
urgency_reason: Venom can cause rapid paralysis, tissue death, or internal bleeding. Seek antivenom immediately.

name: COVID-19
description: An infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.
spreads: Respiratory droplets and small particles when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks.
prevention: Get vaccinated and boosted
Wear a mask in crowded indoor spaces
Maintain social distancing and wash hands frequently
urgency_reason: Can cause severe respiratory distress and long-term health complications.

name: Ringworm
description: A common fungal skin infection; it is not actually caused by a worm.
spreads: Skin-to-skin contact with an infected person or animal, or touching contaminated objects.
prevention: Keep skin clean and dry
Avoid sharing towels, clothing, or hairbrushes
Wear shoes in public locker rooms or showers
urgency_reason: Uncomfortable and contagious, but rarely dangerous to overall health.

name: Scabies
description: An itchy skin condition caused by a tiny burrowing mite called Sarcoptes scabiei.
spreads: Direct, prolonged, skin-to-skin contact with an infected person.
prevention: Avoid direct contact with infected individuals
Wash bedding and clothing of infected persons in hot water
Treat all household members simultaneously if one person is infected
urgency_reason: Intense scratching can lead to secondary bacterial skin infections like impetigo.

name: Conjunctivitis (Pink Eye)
symptoms: redness in the white of the eye
description: Inflammation or infection of the transparent membrane that lines the eyelid and covers the eyeball.
spreads: Direct contact with eye secretions or contaminated hands/surfaces.
prevention: Wash hands frequently
Avoid touching or rubbing the eyes
Do not share towels or eye makeup
urgency_reason: Highly contagious but usually resolves without permanent eye damage if managed.

name: Whooping Cough (Pertussis)
description: A highly contagious respiratory tract infection caused by bacteria.
spreads: Respiratory droplets from coughing or sneezing.
prevention: Vaccinate infants with the DTP/Pentavalent vaccine
Ensure boosters for older children and adults
Keep infected persons away from infants
urgency_reason: Can be life-threatening for infants who may stop breathing (apnea) during coughing spells.
